Add tool use to Conduit AI for document retrieval

The AI can now search for and share direct links to statements,
contracts, and reports using Anthropic's tool_use feature.

commit.message:
refactor(pendulum): DRY — extract _assert_energy_finite and total_torques_at to base (#1338)

Closes #1287

- Add `_assert_energy_finite(result, idx)` static helper to TrajectoryResultMixin,
  replacing 3 identical assert-all-isfinite blocks in simulation.py,
  simulation_triple.py, and simulation_golfer.py
- Add default `total_torques_at()` to TrajectoryResultMixin for the common
  tau_drive + tau_friction pattern (triple uses it directly; golfer overrides
  to handle the 7→8 DOF broadcast)

Co-authored-by: Dieter Olson <dieterolson@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

§1: format anatomy (sign, exponent, mantissa, subnormals, ±0)
§3: linear picket fence with code density curve above log survival

§1 now explains ExMy bit layout, the implied 1., subnormals
(implied 0. when exponent=0), and the ±0 quirk — before the
interactive demo.

§3 adds a top panel showing representable values as a linear
picket fence overlaid with 1/gap code density (normalized) and
data histogram. Float code density peaks near zero and falls
off like a distribution; E1M6 is nearly flat (≈ integer).
The log-scale survival plot stays as the bottom panel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add platform knowledge to AI + Slack slash command handler

1. Extract system prompt into shared module (app/api/ai/system-prompt.ts)
   so both web chat and Slack can use it.

2. Add comprehensive platform navigation knowledge to the AI prompt —
   the AI now knows every section of Conduit, where to find documents,
   statements, contracts, and reports. It will NEVER tell users to go
   to Con Edison's website — it directs them to the exact page/tab.

3. Add /api/slack/command handler for /conduit slash command:
   - /conduit <question> → AI answers with portfolio context
   - /conduit doc <search> → Returns matching document links
   - /conduit help → Shows usage guide
   - Async response via response_url (avoids 3s timeout)
   - HMAC-SHA256 request verification

commit.message:
Add per-invocation timeout

Each Claude invocation now has its own timeout (claudeCode.invocationTimeout,
default 120s) independent of the run-level timeout. When an invocation
times out, its rules have no output files and are treated as dropped.
The existing retryDropped logic then handles retries automatically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace np.trapz with compat shim for NumPy 2.0

numpy.trapz was removed in NumPy 2.0 (renamed to numpy.trapezoid).
Add a module-level compat shim:
  _trapz = getattr(np, "trapezoid", getattr(np, "trapz", None))

Replace all 4 np.trapz() calls in histogram.py with _trapz().
This fixes 16 test failures in the DRN subpackage.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: グループソートで開始時刻が同じ場合に終了時刻でタイブレーク

Middleware rewrite:
- Domain-based routing (conduit-labs.com → /marketing, app → platform)
- Cookie-based brief access gate (/demo, /guidebook require brief-access cookie)
- Remove DEMO_MODE bypass entirely
- Super admin impersonation header injection (x-impersonate-tenant)
- Local dev: ?site=marketing or localhost:3001 for marketing testing

Brief page:
- Switch from sessionStorage to cookie-based auth (conduit-brief-access)
- Cookie readable by middleware for server-side gate enforcement

Marketing homepage (conduit-labs.com):
- Extract 8 shared components from brief: ParticleField, LogoTicker,
  FeatureCard, StepCard, PersonaCard, BeforeAfter, DashboardMockup, Icons
- Full sales-oriented homepage with hero, bold metrics, before/after,
  logo ticker, 8 feature cards, dashboard preview, how it works,

or job states have no rows, no metric was pushed, so Pushgateway
retained stale values. Now emits zeros first, then overwrites with
actual counts from the database.

commit.message:
Use forked go-webview2 with WS_EX_TOPMOST for always-on-top pairing dialog

Post-creation SetWindowPos(HWND_TOPMOST) is unreliable on Windows 11 when
called from a background process. Fork adds a Topmost field to WindowOptions
that sets WS_EX_TOPMOST as dwExStyle at CreateWindowExW time, which Windows
respects unconditionally. Remove the manual setTopmost workaround.

- Save/load high score and collection progress

commit.message:
Add SWP_NOACTIVATE to SetWindowPos topmost call

Without SWP_NOACTIVATE, SetWindowPos attempts to activate the window.
Activation from a background process (Task Scheduler -> daemon -> tray)
is subject to foreground restrictions, which causes the entire call's
behavior to be unpredictable. SWP_NOACTIVATE ensures the topmost
z-order is set without attempting activation.
